scholarships.name: Chevening Scholarship
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers tuition, monthly living stipend, return flights, and other allowances for a one-year UK master's.
benefits: Payment of university tuition fees
Monthly living allowance (stipend), with higher rate for London
Return economy airfare to and from your country of residence
Arrival allowance and departure allowance
Cost of an entry clearance (visa) application
Contribution of up to £75 for TB testing where required
Travel top-up allowance
Access to the Chevening engagement and networking programme
eligibility: Be a citizen of a Chevening-eligible country or territory
At least two years' (2,800 hours) work experience after your undergraduate degree
Hold an undergraduate degree qualifying you for a UK master's
Apply to three eligible UK university courses and secure an unconditional offer from one
Meet the Chevening English language requirement by the required deadline
Commit to returning to your home country for at least two years after the scholarship
Not have previously received UK government or Chevening funding
documentsRequired: Evidence of meeting the English language requirement
scholarships.deadlineNote: Early November annually (applications open early August)
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: At least two years' (2,800 hours) work experience

scholarships.name: Commonwealth Master's Scholarship
scholarships.provider: Commonwealth Scholarship Commission in the UK (CSC), funded by the FCDO
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers full tuition, return airfare, and a living stipend plus additional allowances for a UK master's.
benefits: Full tuition fees paid to the UK university
Approved return airfare from home country to the UK
Monthly living allowance (stipend)
Warm clothing allowance, where applicable
Study travel grant towards study-related travel costs
Child allowance for eligible single-parent scholars
eligibility: Be a citizen of, or granted refugee status by, an eligible developing Commonwealth country
Be permanently resident in an eligible Commonwealth country
Hold at least an upper second class (2:1) honours degree, or a 2:2 plus a relevant postgraduate qualification
Be available to start studies in the UK by the required September start
Be unable to afford to study in the UK without this scholarship
Not have previously studied for a year or more in a high-income country
documentsRequired: Full higher education transcripts (with certified translations if needed)
Proof of citizenship or refugee status
scholarships.deadlineNote: Mid-October annually via the CSC online system

scholarships.name: Commonwealth PhD Scholarship
scholarships.provider: Commonwealth Scholarship Commission in the UK (CSC), funded by the FCDO
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers full tuition, return airfare, and a living stipend plus allowances for doctoral study in the UK.
benefits: Full tuition fees paid to the UK university
Approved return airfare from home country to the UK
Monthly living allowance (stipend)
Warm clothing allowance, where applicable
Study travel grant towards study-related travel costs
Child allowance for eligible single-parent scholars
eligibility: Be a citizen of, or granted refugee status by, an eligible developing Commonwealth country
Be permanently resident in an eligible Commonwealth country
Hold at least an upper second class (2:1) honours degree, or a 2:2 plus a relevant master's
Not already be registered for a PhD or MPhil leading to a PhD before the start of the award
Be unable to afford to study in the UK without this scholarship
Be available to start doctoral studies by the required start date
documentsRequired: Full higher education transcripts (with certified translations if needed)
Proof of citizenship or refugee status
Supporting statement from a proposed UK supervisor
scholarships.deadlineNote: Mid-October annually via the CSC online system
scholarships.durationNote: Up to 3 years PhD (or split-site option)

scholarships.name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters (EMJM)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Consortia of 3+ universities in different countries
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers participation (tuition) costs plus contributions to travel, visa and monthly living costs.
benefits: Participation/tuition costs covered by the scholarship
Monthly subsistence allowance (approx. €1,400/month)
Contribution to travel and installation costs
Contribution to visa costs
Study in at least two different countries
Joint or multiple Master's degree awarded
Open to students from anywhere in the world
eligibility: Hold a bachelor's degree or be in the final year of bachelor studies
Must graduate before the Master's programme begins
Open to students of all nationalities worldwide
Meet the consortium's specific academic entry requirements
Meet language proficiency requirements set by the programme
Not have received another EMJM scholarship previously
documentsRequired: Bachelor's degree certificate / transcript of records
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A chosen programme from the Erasmus Mundus catalogue

scholarships.name: Development-Related Postgraduate Courses (EPOS)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Selected German universities offering EPOS courses
scholarships.fundingSummary: Monthly stipend of €934 (graduates) or €1,300 (doctoral candidates) plus insurance, travel and possible extra allowances.
benefits: Monthly stipend of €934 for graduates / €1,300 for doctoral candidates
Health, accident and personal liability insurance
Travel allowance (unless covered otherwise)
Possible monthly rent subsidy
Possible family allowance
Up to 6-month intensive German language course before studies
Study and research allowance
eligibility: Bachelor's degree (typically 4 years) in a relevant subject
At least two years' professional experience after the degree
National of an eligible developing / newly industrialised country
Academic degree normally not older than 6 years
Not resided in Germany more than 15 months at the application deadline
Upper-third academic performance
Language proof (min. B1 German or required English level)
documentsRequired: Employment certificates proving 2+ years of experience
Certified degree certificates and transcript of records
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: At least two years of relevant work experience
A completed bachelor's degree in a related field

scholarships.name: Study Scholarships – Master Studies for All Academic Disciplines
scholarships.fundingSummary: Monthly stipend (€934 graduate rate) plus health insurance, travel allowance and a study allowance.
benefits: Monthly scholarship stipend (graduate rate, approx. €934)
Health, accident and personal liability insurance
Travel allowance
One-time study/research allowance
Possible monthly rent subsidy
Possible family allowance for accompanying children
Funding for a preparatory German language course
eligibility: Completed first (bachelor's) degree, normally no more than 6 years old at the deadline
Open to foreign graduates of all academic disciplines
Not resided in Germany more than 15 months at the application deadline
Aiming for / admitted to a full Master's programme at a German university
Strong academic record
Language skills meeting the chosen programme's requirements
documentsRequired: Letter of admission / proof from the German university (where required)
One signed academic letter of reference
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A bachelor's degree completed within the last 6 years
A chosen or admitted German Master's programme

scholarships.name: France Excellence Eiffel Scholarship Programme
scholarships.provider: French Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs (via Campus France)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Monthly allowance plus transport, insurance and housing support; tuition not covered.
benefits: Monthly allowance of €1,200 (Master's) or €2,100 (Doctorate) from January 2026
International and domestic transport costs covered
Health and social insurance coverage
Housing assistance
Cultural activities allowance
eligibility: Foreign (non-French) nationality
Applications submitted only by French higher education institutions, not directly by students
Applying for a Master's (M1/M2), engineering, or joint doctoral programme in a priority discipline
Cannot be combined with another French government or Erasmus+ scholarship
New doctoral candidates seeking a 36-month grant must not already be studying in France
Selection based on academic excellence of the nominated student
scholarships.deadlineNote: Institutions submit applications in early January (8 January for the 2026 intake).
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A French host institution willing to nominate you
Admission to or a strong application for an eligible programme
CV and motivation letter ready for the nominating institution

scholarships.name: Swedish Institute Scholarships for Global Professionals (SISGP)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Swedish universities offering eligible master's programmes
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers full tuition, a monthly living allowance, insurance and a travel grant.
benefits: Full tuition fees paid directly to the university
Monthly living allowance of SEK 12,000
One-time travel grant of SEK 15,000
Insurance against illness and accident via Kammarkollegiet
Membership in the SI Network for Global Professionals (NFGP)
Lifetime membership of the SI alumni network
Leadership and professional development activities
eligibility: Citizen of one of the eligible countries on the SI list (approx. 40 countries)
Minimum 3,000 hours of demonstrated work experience
Demonstrated leadership experience and engagement in societal/sustainability issues
Must first apply to an eligible master's programme via universityadmissions.se
Must not have lived in Sweden for two years or more, nor hold a Swedish residence permit
May submit only one scholarship application and hold only one SI scholarship
documentsRequired: Proof of work experience (employer certificates or references)
Proof of application to an eligible master's programme (universityadmissions.se application number)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Scholarship call opens in February, after the universityadmissions.se master's application (mid-January).
scholarships.durationNote: Length of the master's programme (up to 2 years)
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A submitted application to an eligible master's programme via universityadmissions.se
At least 3,000 hours of documented work experience
A strong CV and letter(s) of reference

scholarships.name: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships
scholarships.provider: State Secretariat for Education, Research and Innovation (SERI) – Federal Commission for Scholarships (FCS/ESKAS)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Swiss public universities, the ETH domain and recognised art/music schools
scholarships.fundingSummary: Monthly stipend of CHF 2,450 plus health insurance, flight and transport benefits; tuition fees not covered.
benefits: Monthly stipend of CHF 2,450 for basic living expenses
Health insurance premiums covered (non-EU/EFTA nationals)
One-time rental deposit grant of CHF 600
Half-Fare public transport travelcard
Return airfare to Switzerland (non-EU/EFTA nationals)
Social and cultural activities via the coordinating body
eligibility: Born after 31 December 1991
Master's degree or equivalent completed by 31 July 2027 (research/PhD); Bachelor's for art scholarships
Detailed research/project plan required
Letter of support/acceptance from a Swiss academic supervisor (research scholarships)
Priority to applicants with no prior Swiss study or research experience
Applicant's country must be on the ESKAS eligible list (apply via home country)
Previous ESKAS scholarship recipients are ineligible
documentsRequired: Research proposal on the FCS/ESKAS form (max 5 pages)
Letter of support from a Swiss supervisor plus supervisor's CV
Certified copies of university degrees and transcripts
scholarships.deadlineNote: Deadline varies by country; the call opens around August/September with country deadlines running from autumn to winter.
scholarships.durationNote: Research: 6-12 months; PhD: up to 36 months; Arts: up to 21 months
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A Swiss host professor/supervisor who agrees to support you (research/PhD)
A completed Master's degree (or completion by programme start)
Your country's specific ESKAS deadline and eligible scholarship types
